name: BioXcel Therapeutics, Inc.
business: BioXcel Therapeutics, Inc. is a biopharmaceutical company that develops medicines in neuroscience and immuno-oncology using an artificial intelligence platform. Its approved product, IGALMI (dexmedetomidine) sublingual film, is used for the acute treatment of agitation associated with schizophrenia or bipolar I or II disorder in adults. The company is also developing BXCL501 for agitation in other settings and indications such as Alzheimer's dementia and at-home use.
sellsTo: adults with agitation associated with schizophrenia or bipolar disorder

exposures.sentence: Our most advanced neuroscience candidate is BXCL501. In indications other than those approved by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) as IGALMI , BXCL501 is an investigational, proprietary, orally dissolving sublingual film formulation of dexmedetomidine in development for the treatment of agitation associated with psychiatric and neurological disorders.

Our most advanced immuno-oncology asset, BXCL701, is an investigational oral innate immune activator from OnkosXcel Therapeutics as a potential therapy for the treatment of aggressive forms of prostate cancer, pancreatic cancer, and other solid and liquid tumors.
We developed a proprietary AI platform to reduce therapeutic development costs and potentially accelerate development timelines.

catalysts.event: sNDA submission for IGALMI at-home use for agitation in bipolar disorders or schizophrenia
catalysts.sentence: The sNDA was submitted to the FDA on January 14, 2026.

catalysts.event: SERENITY At-Home Pivotal Phase 3 trial met primary objective (safety)
catalysts.sentence: On August 27, 2025 we announced that the SERENITY At-Home Pivotal Phase 3 trial evaluating the safety of BXCL501, as an acute treatment for agitation associated with bipolar disorders or schizophrenia in the at-home setting met its primary objective.

catalysts.event: Positive topline exploratory efficacy data from SERENITY At-Home trial
catalysts.sentence: On September 10, 2025 we further announced positive topline exploratory efficacy data from the SERENITY At-Home Pivotal Phase 3 safety trial, which demonstrated BXCL501 had continued effects and consistent benefit with repeat dosing.

catalysts.event: Positive results from correlation study for SERENITY At-Home efficacy outcomes
catalysts.sentence: On October 10, 2025 we announced positive results from a correlation study related to exploratory efficacy outcomes from the SERENITY At-Home trial.

catalysts.event: Positive topline results from TRANQUILITY II Phase 3 trial for agitation in Alzheimer's dementia
catalysts.sentence: On June 29, 2023, we announced positive topline results from TRANQUILITY II, a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, parallel group trial that evaluated the safety and efficacy of BXCL501 for the acute treatment of Alzheimer's-related agitation in adults 65 years and older with mild to moderate dementia in assisted living facilities and residential care settings.

catalysts.event: Positive topline results from post-marketing requirement study on repeat dosing of IGALMI 180 mcg
catalysts.sentence: On June 25, 2024, we announced positive topline results from our post-marketing requirement study evaluating whether tolerance, tachyphylaxis, or withdrawal occur following repeat dosing of the 180 mcg (highest approved dose) of IGALMI.

catalysts.event: FDA concluded TRANQUILITY II site inspection closed; Voluntary Action Indicated
catalysts.sentence: On March 3, 2025 we announced that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) concluded that the inspection of a single site in its TRANQUILITY II Phase 3 trial was closed under 21 C.F.R.20.64(d)(3) and released the Establishment Inspection Report.

catalysts.event: Positive topline results from Columbia Phase 2 investigator-sponsored trial in opioid use disorder
catalysts.sentence: On March 5, 2026, the Company announced positive topline results from the Columbia Phase 2 investigator-sponsored trial evaluating BXCL501 for the treatment of opioid withdrawal symptoms in adults with opioid use disorder undergoing a methadone taper.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 1.9 million patients in the United States diagnosed with Alzheimer's disease-related dementia experience agitation at a rate of about six episodes per month on average.
tamClaims.sentence: We estimate that in the United States, there are approximately 1.9 million patients diagnosed with Alzheimer s disease-related dementia ( AAD ) and that those patients experience agitation at a rate of about six episodes per month, on average.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 2.3 million Americans with schizophrenia or bipolar disorders experience agitation at home; 1.8 million may be eligible for BXCL501.
tamClaims.sentence: In addition, we estimate that there are approximately 2.3 million Americans diagnosed with schizophrenia or bipolar disorders who experience agitation at home, of whom 1.8 million may be eligible for treatment with BXCL501.

tamClaims.claim: Patients with schizophrenia or bipolar disorders experience agitation at a rate of about three to four episodes per month on average.
tamClaims.sentence: Based on our recently completed Serenity At Home Trial we believe that those patients experience agitation at a rate of about three four episodes per month, on average.
